data.title: Alibaba dips after White House memo claims it's helping Chinese military target US: report
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) shares dropped 4% during early afternoon trading on Friday after the Financial Times reported on a White House memo claiming the China-based tech and e-commerce giant was helping the Chinese military conduct "operations" against targets in the U.S.

The memo said Alibaba has provided the Chinese government and the People's Liberation Army with access to customer data, including payment records, IP addresses, artificial intelligence-related services, and WiFi information, according to the report. Alibaba also allegedly provided information on software vulnerabilities.

However, Alibaba denied the accusations.

"The claims purportedly based on U.S. intelligence that was leaked by your source are complete nonsense," Alibaba responded, according to the report. "This is plainly an attempt to manipulate public opinion and malign Alibaba."

The memo, which was dated November 1, did not provide details on what or whom in the U.S. had been targeted.

The memo surfaced one day after Anthropic (ANTHRO) released a report of how it had disrupted an AI-fueled cyberattack by hackers based in China back in mid-September.

data.title: First Solar selects South Carolina for new panel manufacturing plant
data.description:
First Solar (FSLR) said Friday it plans to build a new $330 million manufacturing facility in Gaffney, South Carolina, to onshore final production processes for Series 6 Plus modules, with commercial operations expected to begin in H2 2026.

The new plant will increase First Solar's (FSLR) total capacity by 3.7 GW, reaching 17.7 GW of annual nameplate capacity in 2027, while expanding the company's footprint in South Carolina, which already includes a distribution center in Spartanburg County and a longstanding partnership with Inland Port Greer.

The onshored processes will transform thin film solar cells produced by First Solar’s (FSLR) international fleet into fully completed modules, the company said.

The Gaffney plant will join First Solar's (FSLR) three fully vertically integrated manufacturing facilities in Ohio, and one each in Alabama and Louisiana, along with R&D centers in Ohio and California; altogether, the company said it will have invested ~$4.5 billion in American manufacturing and R&D infrastructure since 2019.

data.title: Occidental extends winning streak, shares rise for seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum (OXY) rose 1.83% to $42.79 in the afternoon trade on Friday, marking the seventh consecutive session of gains. The stock has moved higher through the past week, including a 2.51% rise on November 7 and a 1.38% increase on November 6.

The Texas-based oil & gas firm posted mixed third-quarter results earlier this week, with profit coming in better than expected while revenue lagged slightly. The company said it plans to keep production steady next year and lower spending, and noted softer performance in its midstream and marketing business. Occidental has also been in focus after agreeing to sell its OxyChem unit to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7B, a move aimed at cutting debt and improving cash flow.

An analyst said Occidental has room for long-term upside as the company cuts costs, improves breakeven levels and sees new potential in the Barnett Shale. The analyst said the chemicals sale should help lower debt and support the company’s plans. Adding to this, the analyst wrote, “This was probably one of the better operational quarters reported.”

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, it has a “hold" recommendation, scoring 2.96 out of 5. The company earned an A+ in profitability, while a D+ in valuation and growth, a D in momentum and C- in revisions, dragged the overall rating.

While Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish, rating the stock Buy.

On Wall Street, analysts also lean cautious. OXY is rated “Hold”, with seven out of 25 analysts rating the stock Buy or higher, 15 recommending a Hold, while three analysts have a Sell recommendation.

Last month, ScotiaBank upgraded Occidental to Buy from Hold, lifting the price target to $55 from $48. Said, the proposed $9.7 billion OxyChem sale would speed up debt retirement and pull forward share buybacks, while still supporting the dividend.

Recently, OXY has declared a $0.24 per-share quarterly dividend, payable on January 15 to shareholders of record on December 10.

Year to date, the stock is down -13.8%.

data.title: Rate cut doubts will hit AI names that rely on heavy debt funding – Niles Investment’s Dan Niles
data.description:
Dan Niles, Niles Investment Management founder and portfolio manager, raised significant concerns over tech company valuations and AI investment sustainability, warning that not all companies in the artificial intelligence race will succeed.

The tech sector (XLK), (VGT), (IYW) might experience its worst November performance since 2008, with questions emerging about companies’ ability to fund massive AI commitments.

“If you look at the two things that have driven the market, the main one is easing money,” Niles said during an interview with CNBC. “And for some reason, even though the Fed penciled in two more rate cuts this year, that December rate cut is called into question.”

This monetary tightening particularly impacts companies that need to raise substantial debt to fund future AI-related revenues, he said.

The analyst specifically questioned whether OpenAI (OPENAI), which projects losses through 2029, can fulfill its $1.4 trillion in commitments. “Can a company that’s going to exit the year at $20 billion in revenue run rate fund $1.4 trillion in commitments that they have out there? The answer, in my mind, is obviously no.”

By contrast, companies like Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG), (GOOGL) were highlighted for their ability to fund AI infrastructure internally with cash flow.

Accounting practices at major tech companies were also flagged as problematic by Niles, particularly the extension of depreciation schedules despite rapidly advancing technology.

The competitive landscape will likely follow historical tech patterns where only one or two companies dominate each vertical, he said.

“In ecommerce, you’ve got one guy, Amazon (AMZN). In search, you got one guy, that’s Google (GOOG), (GOOGL). In streaming, you’ve got one guy, that’s Netflix (NFLX),” Niles explained, predicting Google will prevail in consumer AI due to its “cash flow machine with more data than anyone in the world,” while Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL) will dominate corporate AI thanks to their established enterprise positions.

data.title: AmpliTech signals $50M revenue target for 2026 as rights offering supports ORAN 5G growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AmpliTech Group, Inc. (AMPG) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Fawad Maqbool highlighted "record performance in the third quarter of 2025 and the 9-month period ending September 30, 2025," citing quarterly revenue of $6.09 million, marking a 115% increase year-over-year, and a gross profit of $2.96 million with a 48.6% gross margin. Maqbool stated that "EBITDA positive of nearly $200,000, reflecting substantial improvement in operating performance and margin expansion. Net loss significantly narrowed to $188,000 compared to a net loss of $1.19 million in Q3 2024." The CEO attributed growth to the integration of 5G ORAN technology and momentum in low-noise amplifiers and 5G infrastructure systems, emphasizing strategic R&D investment and a focus on high-growth markets.
  • Maqbool described the rights offering as an "above market price shareholder-friendly rights offering priced at $4 per unit, providing growth capital for scaling ORAN product lines and expanding domestic production." He explained, "The company increased revenue guidance to at least $25 million for fiscal year 2025... We project positive cash flow from operations and profitability to be achieved in fiscal year 2026."
  • The CEO projected "fiscal year 2026 revenue will be at least $50 million, effectively doubling that of projected record fiscal year 2025 of at least $25 million," and detailed a strategy for 25-30% annual revenue growth through 2030, primarily driven by ORAN 5G agreements with two customers.
  • Maqbool explained that the rights offering provides "choice, fairness and alignment," as all shareholders can participate on equal terms, and noted proceeds will scale production, secure inventories, fund certifications, advance product development, expand go-to-market channels, and maintain strategic flexibility without taking on debt.
  • COO Jorge Flores added, "We do have a mixed list of 4G and 5G products and every single one of them carries a different gross margin. All we can say right now... is that at least we're going to be able to achieve double-digit gross margins on every of our products right now."

Outlook

  • The company increased its revenue guidance to "at least $25 million for fiscal year 2025," representing a 160% year-over-year increase, and anticipates "double-digit gross margins in Q4 2025 and 2026."
  • Maqbool forecasted "positive cash flow from operations and profitability to be achieved in fiscal year 2026, assuming continuation of current order pace and margin recovery."
  • Management expects "to receive follow-on orders from both publicly announced LOIs imminently as well as orders from new customers in 2026," and projects fiscal year 2026 revenue at "at least $50 million."
  • The company aims for "25% to 30% annual revenue growth through 2030."

Financial Results

  • AmpliTech reported quarterly revenue of $6.09 million for Q3 2025 and a gross profit of $2.96 million, with a 48.6% gross margin. EBITDA was nearly $200,000, and net loss narrowed to $188,000.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and accounts receivables totaled nearly $12 million, with zero long-term debt and working capital of $14 million as of September 30, 2025.
  • For the nine months year-to-date 2025, revenue reached $20.7 million, and net loss was reduced to $3.8 million.

Q&A

  • Vishal Mishra, Bard Associates Inc.: "So just want to -- I heard you right, which is that you do expect these rights to be traded when they're distributed, correct?" Fawad Maqbool: "Correct."
  • Mishra: "Do we know -- can you give me a more color on that? Is it like those margins, which has been agreed upon are like the gross margin like in the 20s, 30s, 40s or that's not something you can disclose?" Jorge Flores: "We do have a mixed list of 4G and 5G products and every single one of them carries a different gross margin... we are not going to be able to disclose a specific gross margin information on our products due to competitive nature, of course. But that's why we are saying that at least we're going to be able to achieve double-digit gross margins on every of our products right now."
  • Mishra: "I know historically, you've been close to 30%, 40%. Is that something historically you will be or like because these new products, they will be lower, higher or..." Flores: "As you could see from our results in Q2, in which we had a lot of onetime cost driver, we have recovered very handsomely in Q3 with over 40% gross margins. So we are, of course, going to keep allocating capital... to improve on our margins."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were positive, highlighting momentum and congratulating management, while seeking clarity on tradable rights and margin details.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, using phrases such as "we are turning the corner and getting close to achieving profitability" and emphasizing strategic focus, margin recovery, and growth capital deployment. During Q&A, management was cautious about disclosing granular margin figures but reiterated positive margin trends and operational discipline.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more upbeat, shifting from explanations of margin compression and investment costs to highlighting margin recovery, EBITDA positivity, and a strengthened growth trajectory.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue surged from previous quarters, with Q3 2025 showing $6.09 million compared to prior periods. Gross margin rebounded to 48.6% in Q3 from lower levels in Q2, which management attributed to onetime costs in the earlier period.
  • Outlook shifted from a focus on overcoming initial investment hurdles and cost absorption in Q2 to strong revenue guidance, improved margins, and positive EBITDA in Q3.
  • Management's confidence increased, moving from justifying short-term margin compression to emphasizing operational turnaround and future growth drivers.
  • Analysts' tone moved from probing on order fulfillment and backlog visibility to focusing on financial instruments and margin sustainability as results improved.
  • Strategic priorities evolved from aggressive investment for entry into Tier 1 markets to scaling production, leveraging rights offering proceeds, and capitalizing on newly established LOIs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted that gross margin specifics for individual products could not be disclosed due to competitive reasons. The company also highlighted the need for ongoing operational discipline and efficient capital allocation to maintain margin improvements and meet growth targets.
  • On the strategic front, AmpliTech emphasized the importance of successful execution on LOIs, scaling production, and securing supply chains to deliver on forecasted orders and sustain growth.
  • Analyst questions focused on tradability of rights, margin details, and sustainability of recent margin improvements, indicating an ongoing watch on the company's ability to deliver consistent profitability.

Final Takeaway

AmpliTech Group's Q3 2025 earnings call reflected a robust turnaround, marked by record revenue, a sharp recovery in margins, and a strong focus on scaling 5G ORAN product lines. Management outlined a clear path to profitability and doubled revenue targets for 2026, underpinned by strategic LOIs, a shareholder-centric rights offering, and disciplined operational execution. The company positions itself for continued growth, emphasizing operational leverage, expanding product certifications, and maintaining alignment with shareholders as it targets durable value creation in the years ahead.
